SUMMONS ON COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 200048414), PROOF OF SERVICE ONLY, FILED BY PLAINTIFF BROSNAN, JOHN SERVED DEC-12-2021, PERSONAL SERVICE AS TO DEFENDANT CHIU, RICKY YIN KWOK
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-24-2022 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302,DEFENDANT RICKY CHIU DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO ABATE THIS ACTION IS GRANTED BASED ON THE RULE OF EXCLUSIVE CONCURRENT JURISDICTION. "[W]HEN TWO SUPERIOR COURTS HAVE CONCURRENT JURISDICTION OVER THE SUBJECT MATTER AND ALL PARTIES INVOLVED IN LITIGATION, THE FIRST TO ASSUME JURISDICTION HAS EXCLUSIVE AND CONTINUING JURISDICTION OVER THE SUBJECT MATTER AND ALL PARTIES INVOLVED UNTIL SUCH TIME AS ALL NECESSARILY RELATED MATTERS HAVE BEEN RESOLVED. (CITATIONS OMITTED).") (PLANT INSULATION CO. V. FIBREBOARD CORP (1990) 224 CAL.APP.3D 781, 786-787.) THE ACCIDENT THAT FORMS THE BASIS OF THIS ACTION IS ALREADY BEING LITIGATED IN TOY V. CHIU, ET AL., SAN FRANCISCO SUPERIOR COURT CASE NO. CGC-20-585780 ("TOY ACTION").) TOY'S DISMISSAL OF HER COMPLAINT IN THE TOY ACTION DOES NOT BAR ADJUDICATION OF CHIU'S CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR INDEMNITY AGAINST BROSNAN IN THAT ACTION. BROSNAN MUST SEEK RELIEF FROM THE DEFAULT IN THE TOY ACTION TO LITIGATE HIS DISPUTE WITH CHIU. IF BROSNAN DOES NOT OBTAIN RELIEF FROM THAT DEFAULT, THEN ANY SUBSEQUENT DEFAULT JUDGMENT WILL BAR THIS ACTION BASED ON RES JUDICATA/CLAIM PRECLUSION. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: SUZANNE BOLANOS, CLERK: W. TRUPEK; NOT REPORTED (302/SRB)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-18-2024 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to Government Code Section 68608(b). The court finds no good cause or substantial justification for failure to comply with the previous court orders. A less severe sanction would not be effective due to the history of lack of compliance. Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-19-2022 Continued To Nov-09-22 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-24-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT RICKY CHIU DEMURRER to COMPLAINT

Defendant's motion to abate this action is granted based on the rule of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction. "[W]hen two superior courts have concurrent jurisdiction over the subject matter and all parties involved in litigation, the first to assume jurisdiction has exclusive and continuing jurisdiction over the subject matter and all parties involved until such time as all necessarily related matters have been resolved. (Citations omitted).") (Plant Insulation Co. v. Fibreboard Corp (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 781, 786-787.) The accident that forms the basis of this action is already being litigated in Toy v. Chiu, et al., San Francisco Superior Court Case No. CGC-20-585780 ("Toy Action").) Toy's dismissal of her complaint in the Toy Action does not bar adjudication of Chiu's cross-complaint for indemnity against Brosnan in that action. Brosnan must seek relief from the default in the Toy Action to litigate his dispute with Chiu. If Brosnan does not obtain relief from that default, then any subsequent default judgment will bar this action based on res judicata/claim preclusion.

Order signed.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/SRB)
